Farn Porrrrcal PnecrrcEs CouvussroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 r Sacramento, CA 9SBl4-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
May 27,2004
Michelle E. De Guzman
Community Economic Development
Coordinator
City of Emeryville
1333 Park Avenue
Emeryville, CA 94608
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. 4-04-100
Dear Ms. De Guzrnan:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the conflict-oi
interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the..Act'),r
QUESTTONS
l. May you review, arralyze, and develop recommendations and documents related to the
redevelopment of the Emeryville Marketplace?
2. May you engage in the activities noted above with regard to the redevelopment of Site
B?
CONCLUSIONS
1. It is presumed that your rear property will experiance a material financial effect as a
result of these decision regardii g the Emeryville Marketplace. However, based on the
information you have provided, you may participate in these decisions because the Act,s
"public generally'' exception applies.
2. Because the Commission does not issue advice on past conduct, we are unable to
advise you with regard to this question.
' Go,,emm"nt Code sections 81000 * gl0l4. Commission regulations appear at Title 2, sections
18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations.
4+]F


FileNo. A-04-100
Page No. 2
FACTS
You are a community economic development coordinator in the Economic and
Housing Deparhnent (the "Deparhnent') for the City of Emeryvillg a position you have
held since December 2AO2- The primary function of the Departrnent is to implement the
objectives and goals of the City of Emeryville Redevelopment Agency. 1.ou are also a
resident and homeowner in the City of Emeryville. Youhaverecentlystarted work on
two redevelopment projects which are fairly close to your residence, and you want to
clariff whether or not you have a conflict of interest.
In your capactty as a community economic development coordinator, you review
and analyze projects which may result in the purchasg leasing andlor redeveiopment of
real property. You produce recoillmendations which must be approved by serrior
management and/or the City of Emeryville Redevelopment Agencypriorlo
implementation- You do not have the authorityto contactuallyUina tfre city for any
seryices.
The economic interest which may cause a conflict ofinterestis yourprincipal
residence. It is a condominium unit in the City of Emeryville. Yogr unii is part of a 5g3-
unit, 30-story housing development. You own the unit as a joint tenant with your
husband. You have no otJrer real property interests.
The Emeryville Marketplace is within 500 fu of yourprincipal residence.
Almost all of the development associated with this project would oscgr within the 50O-
foot limit of your home. This project currently entails the appraisaUacquisition of two
properties, which are planned to be integrated into an existing retail center. yotrr role in
this project will include the analysis of the feasibility of the project, day-to-day
management of any consultants involved, review of developmentproposals, and
recorrmendations to the elected City of Emeryville Rdevelopm*t Ag*"yboard.
Redevelopment Site B is over 500 feet away from yourprincipal residence. This
project currently entails the appraisaUacquisition of fiveproperties, wiich are planned to
be redeveloped into a retail or mixed-use development. Your role in this project is
identical to the tasks you would need to perform forthe Emeryville Vtartetpiace project.
You have already performed the task of developing a Requesifor proposats 1..nff,j fo,
the Site B project. You point out that the RFP's evaluation criteria *o" a"r"toped and
in place prior to your employment so that you merely transferred the criteria to the Site B
project.
Emeryville is a total of 1.2 square miles in size and, based on the most current
estimates from the California Deparfrnent of Finance, it has apopulation of 7,675. Based
on data from Metroscan, there are 3,381 residential property o*.r".s in the City of
Emeryville- Of those,757 individuals own residential property units in your housing
development. This is 22o/o of residential property o*r"r, in the city.


File No. ,4-04-100
Page No. 3
Your housing development is a high-rise skucture consisting of 583 condominium
units. Parking is consolidated in a shared parking gilrage, and egres=s and ingress for the
project are through a few corlmon access points. Access is not iegulated based on unit
size, Iocation, etc- Due to the consolidation of these access points and the very dense
nature of your development, impacts from baffic, noise levJs, and air emissions, and any
consequential impacts on property value, will be felt equally by all property owners.
ANALYSIS
The primarypurpose for the conflict-of-interest provisions of the Act is to ensure
that "[p]ublic officials, whether electd or appointed, [siould] perform their duties in an
impartial manner, free from bias caused by thet own financial interests or the financial
interests ofpersons who have supported them." (Section 81001(b).) In furtherance of
this goal, section 87100 of the Actprohibits apublic official tom matcing, participating
in making, or otherwise using his or her official position to influence a go-ve.nmental
decision in which the official has a financial interest_
Determining whether a conflict of interest exists under section 87100 requires
analysis of the following steps as outlinod below.2
steps one and rwo: Are you considered a *public official, and are you making
participating in making or influencing a goyernmental decision?
As a communitSr economic development coordinator ('CED coordinatot') for the
City of Emeryville, you are a "member, officer, employee or consultant of a state or local
govemment agenct''and are, thereforg apublic official subject to the conflict-of-interest
provisions of the Act. (Section 8204g; regulation lg70l(d.)
A public offi"r|l "makes a govemmental decision" when the official, acting
within the authority of his or her office orposition, votes on a matter, obligates or
commits his or her agency to any course of action, or enters into any contricfual
agreement on behalf of his or her agency. (Section 87100; regulation :f7AZJ.) A public
official "participates in making a govemmental decision" whJn, acting within the
authority ofhis or her position and without significant substantive or intervening review,
the official negotiates, advises or makes recofllmendations to the decisionmaker
regarding the governmental decision. (Section 87100; regulation lg712.2-) A public
official is attempting to use his or her official position to tflo*"" a decision before his
9r ler own agency i{, for the purpose of influencing the official contacts or appears
before any member, officer, employee, or consultant ofhis or her agency. lsection
871 00; regulation 15702.3.)
If you, as a CED coordinator, engage in any ofthe actions detailed above with
regard the marketplace redevelopment project, you will ..participate in making,, or
2 Th"se questions are based on the Act's conflict-of-interest analysis provided at regulation t g700(b).


FileNo. 4-04-100
Page No. 4
"influence" that decision-3 Such actions could include negotiating, conducting research,
making any investigation, orpreparingany report to the "ity. tn"gulation I 5702.2(b).)
You have stated that you have already worked on the development of the RFp for
the Site B redevelopment project but point out that the RFP's evaluation criteria were
developed and in place prior to your employment. The Commission considers the RFp to
be an integral part of a conhact and, as such, decisions leading up to the contract, such as
the decision which sets the foundation for the contractual relationship, are decisions
concerning the contract. (Brue Advice Letter, No. I-93-38 4; BrovwrAdvice Letter, No.
A-93-261; In re Thorner (1975) I Fppc ops. l9g.) Thereforg your work on the
development of the RFP constitutes participation in a decision conceming the ultimate
Site B contract- The fact that you selected previously devised RFp proviiions rather than
draft them does not alter this conclusion-
Because the Commission does not issue advice relating to pst conduct
(regulation 18329OXSXA), enclosed), we cannot issue you ua"i".r"itt regard to the Site
B redevelopment project as you have alreadyparticipated in the contract decision.
Step Three: What is your economic fulfsr'esf - 
the possible sources of a conflict of
interest?
Section 87103 provides that apublic official has a "financial interest, in a
govemmental decision "if it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the public generally on t1,e
official, a member of his or her immediate family," or on any oftfr" ofucial's-economic
interests, described as follows:
A public official has an economic interest in a business entity in which he or she
has a direct or indirect invesfinent a of $2,000 or more (section s7lo3(a);
regulation 18703.1(a)); or in which he or she is a director, officer, p*t ra trusteg
employee, or holds any position of management (section s7103(O; regulation
r8703.1(b));
A public official has an economic interest in real property in which he or she has a
direct or indirect interest of $2,000 or more (section szr0r&); regulation
18703.2);
3 Of course, you will "make a govemmental decision" if you vote, obligate or commit the city to
any course of action, or enter into any confractual agreement on beLlf of the cit| on such a decision-
(Regulation l87o2.l)
o An indirect investrnent or interest means any inveshnent or interest owned by the spouse of an
official or by a member of the official's immediate lmily, by an agent on behalf of a public of6cial, or by
a business entity or kust in which the official, the ofiiciai's immedi"ate family, or their agents own directly,
indirectly, or beneficially a l0-percent interest or greater. (Section 87103.) ..Immediate family,, is defined
at section 82029 as an official's spouse and dependent children.


a
FileNo. A-04-100
Page No. 5
A public official has an economic interest in any source of income, including
promised income, which aggregates to $500 or more within 12 months priorlo the
decision (section 87 I 03(c); regulation I 8703.3);
A public official has an economic interest in any source of gifts to him or her if
the gifts aggregate to $340 or more within l2 months prior to the decision (section
871 03(e); regulation I 87 03.4);
A public official has an economic interest in his orherpersonal finances,
including those of his or her immediate family -- this is the "personal financial
eflects" rule (section 87103; regulation I 3703.5)-
a
Assuming you have a direct or indirect interest of $2,000 in your condominium
unit, you have an economic interest in this real propertlrwhich serves as yourprincipal
residence.
You have not provided information regarding any other economic interests- For
purposes of this letter, we assume that you have no other economic interests relevant to
the decision you have identified-
Step Four: fs your economic interest direcfly or indirectly involved in the
governmental decision ?
Real property in which a public official has an rconorric interest is directly
involved in a governmental decision i{, among otherthings,:
"(1) The real property in which the official has an interest,
or any part of that real property, is located within 500 feet
of the boundaries (or the proposed boundaries) of the
property which is the subject of the governmental
decision." (Regulatio n I 87 04.2(aX I ).)
You have stated that your residence is located within 500 feet of the marketplace
development. Therefore, your real property is considered "dtectly involved,'under
regulation 18704.2.
Step Five: \ilhat is the applicable materiality standard?
If the real propefi in which an official has an economic interest is directly
involved in a governmental decision, the materiality standard of regulation ]'37Ai.2(a)
applies. (Regulation 187 04.2(cX I ).)
Regulation 18705.2(a)(l) provides that the financial effect of a governmental
decision on real property (other than a leasehold) which is directly involved in the
goveflrmental decision is presumed to be material. "This presumption may be rebutted
by proof that it is not reasonably foreseeable that the governmental decision will have any


FileNo. A-04-100
Page No. 6
financial effect on the real property." (Ibid.) Please note that "any financial effect"
includes as little as a penny's worth.
Step Six: Is it reasonably foreseeable that the financial effect of the governmental
decision upon your economic interest will meet the applicable materiality standard?
An effect upon economic interests is considered "reasonably foreseeable" if there
is a substantial likelihood that it will o@ur. (Regulation 18706(a).) A financial effect
need not be certain to be considered reasonably foreseeablg but it must be more than a
mere possibility. (In re Thorner (1975) I FPPC Ops. 198.)
It is presumed that the financial effect ofthe decision on yourreal properfy is
material. In order to rebut the presumption of regulation 18705.z(a)(l), you must show
that it is not reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have any financial effect on
your real property. For examplg you would have to show that it is not reasonably
foreseeable that the effect ofredevelopingthe EmeryvilleMarketplace, property in close
proximity to your residencg will not have even a penny's effect on the residence-
The Commission does not act as a finder of fact in providing advica (In re
Oglesby (1975) I FPPC Ops. 71.) Therefore, the determination ofwhether ornot it is
reasonably foreseeable that the applicable materiality standard will be met is necessarily a
factual question that is ultimately for you to decide. However, we reiterate, it is
presumed to be reasonably foreseeable that your condominium unit will experience a
material financial effect as a result ofthe martetplace decision-
Step Seven: The 6'Public Generally" Exception
Even if a public official determines that his or her economic interest will
experience a material financial effect as a result of the decision before the official, he or
she may still participate if the "public geirerally'' exception applies to each person or real
property triggering a conflict of interest. (Regulation 18707(b)(a).) Regulation 18707.1
applies to your situation and provides the requirernents for the general exception:
"(a) Except as provided in Government Code sections
87102.6 and 87103.5, the material financial effect of a
govemmental decision on apublic official's economic
interests is indistinguishable from its effect on the public
generally ifboth subdivisions OXI) and (b)(2) ofthis
regulation apply.
(b) Significant Segments and Indistinguishable Effects.
(l) Significant Segment. The governmental decision will
affect a 'significant segment' of the public generally if any
of the following are affected as set forth below:
fl...1t
(B) Real Property. For decisions that affect a public
official's real property interest, the decision also affects:


File No. 4-04-100
Page No. 7
(i) Ten percant or more of all property owners or all
homeowners in the jurisdiction ofthe official,s agency or
the district the official represents; or
(ii) 5,000 property owners orhomeowners in the
jurisdiction of the official's agency.
1r...11
(2) Substantially the Same Manner: The governmental
decision will affect a public official,s economic interest in
substantially the same manner as it will affect the
significant segment identified in subdivision (b)(l) ofthis
regulation-"
You have stated that there are 3,381 residential property owners in the City of
Emeryville, If these property owners equate to either all property owners or all
homeowners in the city, then a "significant segment" of the "public generallyp exception
would be affected by the marketplace rdevelopment projecfsin ". lOyoor more (i.i.,
22%) of the propertylhomeowners vrin be affectod. (Regulation lgT0T.lOXlXBXi).)
- In addition, you have provided facts pertaining to the nature of your housing
development and potential impacts from the marketplace redevelopment project, and
specifically mention impacts on traffic, noise levels, air emissions, ana prtperty values.
Based on your facts, due to the consolidation of entrypoints and the ""y a."*"nafure of
your development, the financial impacts from the marketplaceproject will be
substantially the same for owners in your building. Consequen-tly, it upp"*r that the
marketplace decision will affect yourreal prcpertyin substantialiythe-samemanner as it
will affect a significant segment of the public generally. As a result, the..public
generally'' exception applies, and you may participate in the marketplace iedevelopment
decisions you have identified-
Step Eight: (Legally Required Participation, Exception
Facts you have presented do not suggest that the "legally required participation,,
exception to the conflict-of-interest rules is applicable to the situation.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-566A.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
Bocanegra
Enclosure
NBje

Counsel, Legal Divi sion
